stimulus: Tardigrades are microscopic animals famous for surviving extreme conditions, from boiling springs to the vacuum of space. They are not truly ______ in fact, many populations endure long stretches of dormancy in which they expel nearly all moisture from their bodies and curl into a desiccated form known as a tun.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The comma correctly separates the supplementary adverb "however" from the preceding main clause ("They are not truly invincible"), and the semicolon after "however" correctly joins the two main clauses ("They…however" and "many…tun"). Placing the semicolon after "however" signals that the second clause expresses a contrast to what the previous sentence implies.

Choice B is incorrect because it produces a comma splice: a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction. Choice C is incorrect because placing the semicolon after "invincible" misaligns the contrast logic, treating "however" as part of the second clause instead of the supplementary adverb to the first. Choice D is also a comma splice; the two main clauses are joined only by a comma.
